subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative structure with logical progression through introduction, derivations, data fitting, evaluation and conclusion
Comprehensive table of contents, numbered figures/tables and proper cross-referencing enhance readability
Mostly relevant and concise content focusing on the research question
weaknesses: Two figures lack captions and in-text references
Minor typos and an incomplete concluding sentence undermine polish
Variable citation formatting across the bibliography
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and well organized, with a clear structure and mostly relevant content. Occasional lapsesâmisplaced discussions, formatting inconsistencies, missing captions and minor typosâprevent it from achieving the highest band. Band 3 (score 3) accurately reflects strong coherence and organization tempered by these flaws.

strengths: Clear step-by-step derivation of the exponential model via separation of variables
Effective linearization of data with well-labelled tables and scatter plots
Good logical flow connecting formulae, diagrams and prose
weaknesses: Inconsistent use of ln vs log and dropping of absolute values without justification
Symbol r is used ambiguously for both growth constant and correlation coefficient
No residual plots or error bars to assess model fit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly consistent, and multiple representations (tables, plots, regression equations) are used effectively. Minor notation slips and the absence of residual analysis limit clarity and precision. These features align with Band 3 (score 3).

strengths: Strong personal connection to Moray Firth and conservation motivation
Independent choice of a local dolphin population and derivation of two models
weaknesses: Modelling approach remains largely conventional without novel twists
Key assumptions (e.g. carrying capacity) lack justification or creative analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is clear evidence of personal engagement and significant independent thinking, but the investigation follows standard methods and offers limited creativity. This aligns with Band 2 (score 2).

strengths: Meaningful discussion of model limitations and realistic extensions
Thoughtful qualitative analysis of advantages and disadvantages of each model
Clear evaluation of outcomes against observed data
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative error analysis or statistical measures of fit
Does not justify omission of external factors or probe negative logistic slope deeply
Reflection remains descriptive rather than deeply critical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis, but critical insight is limited and no deep error evaluation is conducted. This corresponds to Band 2 (score 2).

strengths: Differential equations, separation of variables and regression analysis are applied effectively
Mathematical methods are commensurate with HL course level
Derivations and model applications serve the research question purposefully
weaknesses: Some algebraic slips and arithmetic errors in formula implementation and tables
Carrying capacity assumption (M = 250) is unsupported and sensitivity analysis is absent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant, correctly applied and appropriate to the course level, demonstrating a solid understanding. Minor errors and unsupported assumptions prevent the highest score. These attributes align with Band 4 (score 4).

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations generally flow, connecting formulae, diagrams and prose so the reader can follow computations.  Minor logical gaps (e.g., unexplained sign of the logistic slope, abrupt figure insertions) limit absolute clarity, but the argument remains understandable.  Hence âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ (Band 3) rather than âexcellentâ.

Derivations and regression calculations are mainly correct; only minor slips (e.g., unexplained negative r in logistic fit, algebraic typo in final formula) are noted and do not undermine the overall validity.  Demonstrated understanding is good, but not entirely âthorough and completely correctâ, placing it in Band 4.

The student employs tables of raw and transformed data, scatter plots with regression lines, and final model equations, enabling the reader to visualise results from multiple perspectives.  Representations are accurate and clearly labelled, but residual plots or error bars are absent, so use is effective rather than âhighly effectiveâ; Band 3 is appropriate.

The report follows a clear narrative: introduction â derivations â data fitting â evaluation â conclusion.  Each stage builds logically on the previous one and the student rarely digresses from the central aim.  There are occasional lapses (e.g., a misplaced discussion of PMCC under the logistic section), but the mathematical development remains understandable without rereading sections out of order.  Overall coherence is good rather than exemplary, justifying Band 3.

Some initiative is shown in compiling and transforming data, but creativity is limited; assumptions (e.g., M = 250) are made without justification, and no alternative techniques or original visualisations are attempted.  This reflects âsome creativity or initiativeâ, hence Band 1.

Critical analysis exists: correlation coefficients are interpreted, and advantages/disadvantages of each model are discussed.  However, the analysis is qualitative; statistical measures of goodness-of-fit or parameter sensitivity are omitted.  The work is therefore âthoughtfulâ but not âdeep and insightfulâ â Band 2.

Outcomes are compared with observed data in a consolidated table and general trends are described.  The student comments on long-term vs short-term accuracy and acknowledges dependence on Pâ, demonstrating a clear if basic evaluation.  There is no quantitative error analysis or future prediction interval, so Band 2 is the best match.

The student reflects on model suitability, lists limitations and proposes realistic extensions (adding environmental variables, improving M).  Reflection is present throughout but remains mostly descriptive; it stops short of probing why residuals behave as they do.  Thus the work meets âmeaningful reflectionâ (Band 2).

Variables are symbolised consistently (P, t, r, M), calculus notation is correct and logarithmic manipulation is shown.  A few slipsâswitching between ln and log, ambiguous use of |P| then dropping absolute value without commentâshow that accuracy is not flawless.  Overall, appropriate language is âmostly consistentâ, fitting Band 3.

The introduction links the topic to the studentâs personal experience at Moray Firth, and the evaluation references conservation interest, demonstrating a personal approach.  Yet the modelling route remains conventional and could have been tailored more innovatively (e.g., estimating carrying capacity statistically).  Band 2 fits âclear evidence of personal approachâ.

Most material is relevant to modelling dolphin populations; extraneous textbook explanations are limited and the 22-page length is acceptable for HL.  Some repetition occurs (e.g., definition of PMCC appears twice) and long verbatim derivations of standard results could have been abbreviated for conciseness, so the exploration is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly conciseâ.

Differential equations, separation of variables, logarithmic linearisation, regression and PMCC are all directly relevant to population modelling and are applied correctly.  The mathematics is employed purposefully to answer the research question, satisfying the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ descriptor (Band 4).

Selecting a local dolphin population and independently deriving two models shows genuine involvement beyond textbook replication.  However, the work largely follows standard procedures without novel analytical twists; independent thinking is âsignificantâ, not âoutstandingâ, placing it in Band 2.

Headings, a table of contents, numbered tables/figures and a sensible progression of sections demonstrate solid structure.  Cross-referencing is generally correct and helps the reader locate information quickly.  Minor flawsâsuch as two figures inserted without captions in the middle of a paragraphâprevent it from reaching the polish expected in Band 4, but the document is unmistakably well organised.

Solving first-order differential equations and deriving logistic form is squarely at HL level.  While not exceptionally sophisticated, the work is entirely commensurate with the syllabus and appropriately applied, aligning with Band 4 (âfully commensurate with course levelâ).

annotations.comment: The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly structured, making navigation through the exploration effortless. This enhances the organization and supports logical development.
The studentâs personal connection to Moray Firth and conservation enthusiasm clearly demonstrate personal engagement, making the investigation feel authentic and motivated.

The decision to ignore external factors is stated but not justified. The student should explain why environmental or human influences are omitted and discuss limitations this assumption imposes on model validity.
The separation of variables and integration to derive $$P=Ae^{rt}$$ is presented clearly, with each algebraic step shown. This demonstrates good mathematical communication.
When dropping the absolute value from $$\ln|P|$$ to $$\ln P$$, the explanation is minimal. The student should clarify why $$P>0$$ justifies removing the bars and ensure consistent notation.
The linearization of the exponential model via $$\ln P(t)=\ln P_0 + rt$$ is well explained, clearly linking the data transformation to the regression context.
The regression constant is reported to many decimal places (e.g. 0.0338754385965). Rounding to three significant figures would improve readability without compromising accuracy.
The symbol \(r\) is used both for the growth constant and the PMCC. To prevent confusion, reserve a different symbol (e.g. \(\rho\) or \(r_c\)) for the correlation coefficient.
The absence of a residual plot or error bars limits your assessment of model fit. Including residual analysis would strengthen the evaluation of linearity and homoscedasticity.
The notation for \(t\) in Table 2 varies dramatically (bold, fraktur, overline). Consistent formatting is essential for clarity and professional presentation.
The carrying capacity assumption \(M=250\) is not supported by data. The student should justify this choice statistically or perform a sensitivity analysis to assess its impact.
The explanation of setting \(A=\pm e^c\) and then requiring \(A>0\) is confusing. Clarify how the constant emerges from integration and why negative values are excluded.
In the linearization of the logistic model, the original population symbol is lowercase \(p\) in one instance. Ensure variable cases remain consistent to avoid ambiguity.
The negative slope in the logistic regression indicates decay in \(\ln\,(MP_0/P-P_0)\). Reflect critically on why the growth constant appears negative and what that implies for the model.
Two figures on page 16 lack captions and in-text references. Every graph should have a descriptive caption and be referred to by number in the prose.
The substituted expression in the formula on page 16 includes a value \(230.170\) without derivation. Recheck the arithmetic when substituting into the logistic model to ensure accuracy.
In Table 4, the logistic formulaâs numerator appears as \(250-140\) instead of \(250\times140\). This alters predicted valuesâcheck the implementation of the model formula.
Consolidating both modelsâ predictions against actual data in one table aids direct comparison and strengthens the evaluation of long-term accuracy and trends.
The advantages/disadvantages list provides thoughtful insight into model strengths and limitations, reflecting critical analysis of both mathematical and ecological aspects.
There are minor typos in list items (e.g. âvieldâ and âbottle dolphinsâ). Proofreading is needed to maintain professionalism and clarity.
The conclusion on page 20 ends mid-sentence. Ensure that the concluding remarks are complete and summarize key findings and implications succinctly.
The extension suggestions regarding environmental variables, updated datasets, and empirically determining carrying capacity show strong reflection and provide a clear path for further research.
Citation formatting and indentation vary across entries. Adopting a consistent referencing style (e.g. MLA or APA) would improve presentation and credibility.
Consider including units for population size (e.g. number of dolphins) and time (years) wherever variables are introduced or graphed to reinforce clarity.
The student uses \(\ln\) consistently but occasionally writes âlog.â For precision, choose one notation and note any base differences.
